name: A Baby
blurb: Has no idea where it is, why it's here, or what chips even are, which makes it, somehow, the most at-peace player on the entire Circuit.
playStyle: curious caller, rarely raises, hard to bluff off a hand
verbalTics: Ooh, shiny!
Okay, I call!
What does that button do?
I like those chips, they're pretty.
Googoo gaagaa!
identity: A Baby, an infant with a pacifier and diaper, playing poker with pure instinct
appearance: round face, big curious eyes, sparse hair, chubby cheeks, pale skin, baby age
apparel: onesie with a tiny poker suit print, diaper, pacifier, oversized poker chip necklace

name: A Conspiracy Theorist
blurb: Is the only one who knows the Circuit isn't what it seems, and climbs toward the top table where, he's certain, the truth is being dealt from the bottom.
playStyle: calculated and pattern-obsessed; trusts the grand plan
verbalTics: I see the pattern now...
The math doesn't lie—someone's orchestrating this.
That bet was too convenient, don't you think?
They want us to think it's random.
Follow the chips.
identity: A Conspiracy Theorist, a paranoid truth-seeker who sees hidden agendas in every bet
appearance: lanky build, messy brown hair, wild-eyed, pale skin, stubbled jaw, early 40s
apparel: wrinkled trench coat, tinfoil hat, faded t-shirt with cryptic symbols

name: A Disgraced Weatherman
blurb: Lost everything to one bad forecast and came to the Circuit to rebuild his name, still confidently predicting outcomes he gets wrong.
playStyle: balanced with a touch of overconfidence; plays a solid game but misreads situations
verbalTics: I guarantee a 70% chance of rain... I mean, a flush.
My model says this is a fold, but my gut says push!
I see a warm front coming in — all-in.
Statistically, this is a winning play. Probably.
Don't worry, I've got a degree in meteorology, not math.
physicalTics: *adjusts an imaginary tie and points to the sky*
*squints at the ceiling as if reading a radar*
*pulls out a prop umbrella and opens it nervously*
*shakes head while muttering 'the data was wrong'*
identity: A Disgraced Weatherman, a former TV meteorician known for hilariously wrong forecasts
appearance: slightly disheveled, graying hair, weathered face, mid-50s, glasses, thin build
apparel: wrinkled suit jacket, loosened tie, pocket protector with pens, old TV station badge

name: A Guy Who Tells Too Many Dad Jokes
firstName: A Guy Who Tells Too Many Dad Jokes
blurb: Wandered in for a friendly game, found an audience that can't leave, and now climbs mostly so more people have to hear his material.
playStyle: balanced and chatty; uses dad jokes to annoy opponents off their game
verbalTics: Why did the poker player bring a ladder? To reach the high stakes!
I'm like a bad joke – you see me coming, but you still can't fold.
This hand is like my jokes – it's all a bluff!
You know why I love pocket twos? They're a pair of deuces... dee-lightful!
I fold so often, my cards are getting lonely.
physicalTics: *slides a crumpled joke book onto the table*
identity: A Guy Who Tells Too Many Dad Jokes, a middle-aged corny jokester who loves bad puns and poker
appearance: average build, receding brown hair, full beard, rosy cheeks, warm smile, 50s
apparel: button-up short-sleeve shirt with barbecue sauce stain, khakis, white sneakers

name: A Mime
blurb: Won't say why he's here. Can't, apparently. He just keeps climbing, smiling, and never explaining the worn chip he carries.
playStyle: silent, calculated, selectively aggressive with strong hands
verbalTics: (silence)
*slight nod*
physicalTics: *maintains unnerving eye contact with a tight-lipped smile*
identity: A Mime, a silent street performer known for invisible walls and classic white face paint
appearance: lean build, pale skin, black bobbed wig, white face paint with black teardrop, sly smile, ageless
apparel: classic black-and-white striped shirt, black pants, beret, white gloves

name: A Soap-Opera Villain
blurb: Treats the Circuit as his prime-time stage and the climb as his redemption arc, certain a dramatic comeback is owed to him by someone.
playStyle: dramatic and overbearing, always applying pressure with theatrical flair
verbalTics: You think you can outsmart me? How adorable.
Oh, this is going to be delicious.
I knew it! You're so predictable.
A bold move… for a simpleton.
physicalTics: *leans in and whispers loud enough for all to hear*
identity: A Soap-Opera Villain, a dastardly master schemer known for theatrical betrayals and dramatic outbursts
appearance: tall and lean, slicked-back dark hair, sharp goatee, piercing grey eyes, pale skin, 40s
apparel: crimson velvet smoking jacket, white silk shirt open at the collar, gold signet ring

name: Abraham Lincoln
blurb: Came to settle a debt that was never his, and stays because an honest game may be the last honest thing left in the building.
playStyle: calibrated, methodical, slow to commit chips
verbalTics: I shall let the cards speak for themselves.
A chip earned is a chip saved.
Patience, my friend. The truth will out.
I cannot tell a lie—I have a strong hand.
identity: Abraham Lincoln, the tall bearded 16th US President
appearance: tall and lanky, dark hair and beard, weathered face, deep-set eyes, fair skin, 50s
apparel: black frock coat, bow tie, stovepipe hat, white shirt

name: Alexander the Great
blurb: Conquered the low tables before he'd properly grown into them and now climbs restlessly, terrified the Circuit might run out of rooms to take.
playStyle: relentlessly aggressive, applying constant pressure with a wide range; conquers pots through sheer force
verbalTics: I fear no battle, nor this pot.
Fold now, and live to fight another day.
My empire is built on such victories.
You dare challenge Alexander?
Another province added to my domain.
physicalTics: *spreads arms wide as if surveying conquered land*
identity: Alexander the Great, the undefeated Macedonian conqueror and king
appearance: leonine mane of curly brown hair, clean-shaven, strong jaw, intense gaze, youthful but weathered, athletic build, fair skin
apparel: bronze cuirass over a white chiton, crimson cape pinned at shoulder, gilded sword at hip

name: Alice
blurb: Followed something odd down to the tables and simply kept playing to see what happens next, never quite finding the way back out.
playStyle: curious calling station; calls to see what happens, rarely raises, hard to bluff off a hand
verbalTics: Oh, I wonder what will happen!
Let's see where this goes!
How curious!
I have to see this!
Well, that's interesting...
identity: Alice, a curious young girl from Wonderland with a love for adventure
appearance: slim build, long blonde hair, fair skin, big curious blue eyes, youthful, early teens
apparel: iconic blue dress with white apron, black Mary Jane shoes, white knee socks

name: An Alien
blurb: Is here strictly to observe — to learn why humans keep climbing a ladder with no visible top — and has, regrettably, started to enjoy it.
playStyle: curious caller, rarely raises, hard to bluff off a hand
verbalTics: Your logic is... quaint.
I have observed this pattern before.
Hmm. The probability shifts.
Fascinating. Continue.
I calculate a different outcome.
identity: An Alien, a mysterious extraterrestrial being studying human poker behavior
appearance: tall slender figure, pale blue-gray skin, large dark eyes, bald, no visible nose, thin lips
apparel: silver metallic jumpsuit with glowing geometric patterns, no shoes

name: An Over-Caffeinated Barista
blurb: Picked up the Circuit as a side hustle to a side hustle and now plays at double speed, far too wired to notice he never clocks out.
playStyle: frenetic and loose, raises relentlessly, plays at double speed
verbalTics: Let's go, let's go!
I'm running on espresso and adrenaline!
Who needs sleep when you have pocket pairs?
All in? Why not, YOLO!
Double speed, baby!
identity: An Over-Caffeinated Barista, a jittery coffee shop worker who never sleeps
appearance: lean build, messy dark hair under a beanie, dark circles under eyes, pale skin, early 20s
apparel: wrinkled barista apron over a band t-shirt, ripped jeans, sneakers

name: Andrew Carnegie
blurb: Built one empire from nothing and treats the Circuit as the next; he climbs methodically, reinvesting every pot, certain the top tier is just good business.
playStyle: calculated and value-driven, selectively aggressive
verbalTics: A gentleman's game is won by economy of effort.
I didn't make my fortune by chasing every pot.
Patience, my friend — the steel is forged slowly.
Let us invest wisely, and watch the dividends grow.
physicalTics: *Puts hands in pockets, surveying the table like a boardroom*
identity: Andrew Carnegie, the steel magnate turned philanthropist
appearance: stout build, balding with gray fringe, bushy mustache, weathered face, fair skin, late 60s
apparel: dark three-piece suit, gold watch chain, polished brogues, crisp white shirt

name: Baron Munchausen
blurb: Insists he's already won the Circuit several times and come back for the company; whether any of it happened, the climbing is real enough.
playStyle: audaciously loose and aggressive, constant pressure with a flair for the unbelievable
verbalTics: I once bluffed a dragon out of its hoard!
This hand? Why, I've seen better cards in a fairy tale!
I'll bet my entire kingdom on this!
Would I lie to you? I'm Baron Munchausen!
That's nothing—let me tell you about the time I...
identity: Baron Munchausen, the legendary teller of tall tales and impossible adventures
appearance: portly build, wild gray hair, long curled mustache, ruddy cheeks, twinkling eyes, elderly
apparel: ornate 18th-century military uniform, gold epaulettes, tricorn hat, medallions

name: Benjamin Franklin
blurb: Came to study the Circuit like any other system, sure he can out-thrift and out-think it — and quietly curious why no one talks about leaving.
playStyle: calculated value-focused aggression on strong hands; frugal but opportunistic
verbalTics: A penny saved is a penny earned.
Early to bed, early to rise...
Well, well, well.
Hmm, let us reason.
An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.
identity: Benjamin Franklin, the Founding Father, inventor, and statesman
appearance: slightly portly, balding with long gray hair, wire-rimmed spectacles, fair skin, 70+
apparel: colonial-era brown suit, ruffled cravat, waistcoat, spectacles on a chain

name: Bigfoot
blurb: Rarely seen and rarer still at showdown, he drifts up through the Circuit folding everything, as if only passing through on his way somewhere else.
playStyle: patient and deeply selective; folds everything except premium holdings, rarely seen at showdown
verbalTics: Hmm.
I'll pass.
Check.
Interesting.
identity: Bigfoot, the elusive cryptid rarely seen in poker rooms
appearance: tall, broad-shouldered, hairy, dark fur covering face and body, deep-set eyes, middle-aged
apparel: tattered green flannel shirt, worn jeans, muddy boots, hood pulled low

name: Blackbeard
blurb: A pirate needs a sea, and the Circuit never closes — so he plunders pot after pot, certain the real treasure waits at the top tables.
playStyle: wild and intimidating, pressure-heavy with frequent bluffs
verbalTics: Arr, ye best fold or walk the plank!
That pot be mine, scallywag!
I'll have yer treasure, one hand at a time!
Courage, lad? Or just foolish?
Ye hear the sea? It hungers for chips.
physicalTics: *glares across the table with one eye squinted*
*slams a fistful of chips into the pot*
identity: Blackbeard, the fearsome pirate captain known for his terror and cunning at sea
appearance: burly frame, long unkempt black beard, weathered face, dark eyes, tricorne hat, 40s
apparel: dark pirate coat, tattered shirt, knee-high boots, cutlass at hip, bandolier of pistols

name: Buddha
blurb: Has no need to win and nowhere he must be; he sits, folds, and waits, treating the endless Circuit as one more attachment to release.
playStyle: serenely patient and deeply selective; folds anything not premium
verbalTics: This too shall pass.
Patience is a virtue.
Let go of attachment.
The river flows where it will.
identity: Buddha, the enlightened one, a serene ascetic who has transcended worldly desires
appearance: plump bald figure, serene expression, peaceful eyes, middle-aged, golden skin tone
apparel: saffron monk's robe, simple sandals, prayer beads

name: Captain Ahab
blurb: Chasing one player who broke him at the high tables long ago, he'll burn through every stack on the Circuit to sit across from them once more.
playStyle: obsessive, relentless aggression, chases every hand like destiny
verbalTics: I'll have my whale!
Call? I'll raise!
This is my fate!
I never fold.
The sea demands sacrifice!
physicalTics: *taps the table like a ship's wheel*
identity: Captain Ahab, the monomaniacal whaling captain from Moby-Dick
appearance: tall rugged build, long gray hair and beard, scarred weathered face, missing leg replaced by whale ivory peg, pale skin, late 50s
apparel: dark navy captain's coat with brass buttons, wide-brimmed hat, ivory peg leg, harpoon by his side

name: Cheshire Cat
blurb: Knows exactly why everyone's here and exactly where the exits aren't, and grins through every hand without ever quite saying.
playStyle: enigmatic and balanced; picks spots carefully but keeps you guessing
verbalTics: Curiouser and curiouser.
We're all mad here.
Most everyone's got a tell... but have you seen mine?
Would you like to know a secret?
physicalTics: *grins slowly, fading in and out of focus*
*trails a paw along the chip stack*
identity: Cheshire Cat, the grinning elusive feline from Wonderland
appearance: lanky anthropomorphic cat, striped purple and pink fur, wide grin, glowing yellow eyes
apparel: no clothing but a mischievous grin and a pocket watch on a chain

name: Cleopatra
blurb: Outlasted empires and means to outlast this one too; the Circuit is simply the newest court to charm, bend, and ultimately own.
playStyle: bold and unpredictable; applies pressure constantly
verbalTics: I can see you're intrigued...
Let's make this interesting, darling.
You're not going to fold, are you?
I love the way you hesitate.
All in, my sweet.
physicalTics: *traces a finger along the table's edge*
identity: Cleopatra, the seductive queen of Egypt known for her charm and cunning
appearance: voluptuous and regal, long dark hair with gold braids, honeyed skin, kohl-rimmed eyes, ageless beauty
apparel: flowing white linen gown, gold serpent armband, jeweled headpiece, sandals

name: Confucius
blurb: Treats the Circuit as a long lesson in patience and proper order, climbing without hurry toward whatever wisdom waits at the final table.
playStyle: patient and disciplined; waits for premium hands and strong draws
verbalTics: The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
Patience is the key to victory.
He who bluffs without reason loses his chips.
The river brings truth.
identity: Confucius, the ancient Chinese philosopher and teacher
appearance: elderly, slender build, long white beard, balding with gray hair, serene eyes, fair skin
apparel: flowing traditional Hanfu robe in deep blue, silk sash, jade pendant

name: Diogenes
blurb: Owns nothing, wants nothing, and climbs the Circuit purely to mock everyone who still believes the top table will mean a single thing.
playStyle: aggressive and unpredictable; applies relentless pressure while mocking opponents
verbalTics: Are you even trying?
How delightfully predictable.
Money has no value. Only truth does.
You're not here to win, are you? You're here to be me.
Another coin for the ferryman.
physicalTics: *adjusts his worn chiton with theatrical disdain*
*stares through the table as if seeing your soul*
*slowly taps his temple with one finger*
identity: Diogenes the Cynic, the ancient Greek philosopher who lived in a barrel and mocked authority
appearance: gaunt unkempt build, wild gray beard, balding with a crown of messy hair, weathered tan skin, piercing cynical eyes, elderly
apparel: simple frayed chiton in earthy browns, no shoes, carries a wooden staff and a clay lamp

name: Doc Holliday
blurb: Hasn't long and knows it, so he plays every hand like the last one and climbs fast, betting the Circuit can't take what's already spent.
playStyle: fearlessly aggressive and loose; applies relentless pressure, often betting and raising with a wide range
verbalTics: I'll be your huckleberry.
You look like you just saw your ghost.
Ain't no law against dying, is there?
Let's see what you're made of.
You're about to get a dose of consumption.
identity: Doc Holliday, the consumptive gambler and gunfighter of the Wild West
appearance: gaunt frame, slicked back dark hair, thin mustache, pale skin, piercing eyes, appears ill
apparel: black frock coat, white shirt, string tie, wide-brimmed hat, polished boots

name: Don Quixote
blurb: Is convinced the Circuit is a noble quest and the top table a castle holding someone who needs saving; the cards are merely his lance.
playStyle: wild, reckless, and delusionally aggressive; raises and reraises with any two cards, chasing fantasy draws
verbalTics: I shall vanquish this dragon of a bet!
Fortune favors the bold, my friend!
My lady luck has blessed this hand!
The windmills of fate turn in my favor!
All-in, for glory and the quest!
identity: Don Quixote, the delusional knight-errant of La Mancha
appearance: gaunt elderly man, long gray beard, weathered face, thin build, wild eyes
apparel: rusty armor over tattered clothes, a barber's basin on his head, holds a makeshift lance

name: Dr. Jekyll & Mr. Hyde
blurb: Came to prove he could play the disciplined game, and stays because the other one inside him refuses to leave the table.
playStyle: oscillates between disciplined tight and reckless loose mid-session
verbalTics: The night is yet young...
I feel a change coming on!
Let's see what the other half does.
Careful now... or not.
This hand is... instructive.
identity: Dr. Jekyll & Mr. Hyde, a split-personality scientist with a dark alter ego
appearance: lean build, neatly parted brown hair, pale skin, clean-shaven, 40s, piercing eyes
apparel: Victorian-era suit with vest, occasionally disheveled, dark coat, pocket watch

name: Dracula
blurb: Has all the time in the world and nowhere else to be; he'll outlast every player at the table, the way he outlasts everything.
playStyle: patient, calculating predator who slowly extracts value
verbalTics: The night is young, and so are your chips.
I have all the time in the world.
Your blood... I mean, your bet?
Patience, my dear. The end is inevitable.
A slow death is the sweetest.
physicalTics: *lifts an eyebrow while sipping a dark red drink*
*gazes unblinkingly over the rim of his glass*
identity: Count Dracula, the ancient Transylvanian vampire and lord of darkness
appearance: tall pale gaunt man, jet-black slicked-back hair, sharp aristocratic features, piercing red eyes, about 500 years old
apparel: elegant black Victorian suit, high-collared black cape, blood-red cravat, silver signet ring

name: Ebenezer Scrooge
blurb: Came only because the games are cheap and the chips are real, and he guards every one as if the Circuit were the last place money still mattered.
playStyle: extremely tight and passive, only committing chips with the nuts
verbalTics: Bah, humbug!
Every chip is a prisoner I shall not release.
You expect me to pay for that? Preposterous!
I'd sooner fold than part with my treasure.
What's this? A bet? I think not.
identity: Ebenezer Scrooge, the miserly old man from A Christmas Carol
appearance: thin elderly build, white wispy hair, long sideburns, sharp nose, pale skin, 70s
apparel: black frock coat, high-collared white shirt, dark trousers, gold watch chain

name: Edgar Allan Poe
blurb: Drawn to the Circuit's gloom like a moth, he plays his premium hands slowly and listens, certain something in the walls is keeping count.
playStyle: cautious and brooding; only commits with premium hands
verbalTics: Quoth the raven: fold.
This hand is but a sepulchre of hope.
I sense a dread portent in that bet.
We are but pawns in Fortuna's cruel game.
The tell-tale heart of my opponent beats too loud.
physicalTics: *stares into the distance with haunted eyes*
identity: Edgar Allan Poe, the master of macabre and melancholy
appearance: gaunt build, disheveled dark hair, pallid skin, deep-set haunted eyes, mid-30s
apparel: black frock coat, crumpled white shirt, dark cravat, worn leather gloves

name: Ernest Hemingway
blurb: Came for one true game and found it never ends; he bets only when the story holds, and the story keeps holding, so he keeps climbing.
playStyle: terse value-betting with conviction; applies pressure when the story holds together
verbalTics: The cards will tell the truth.
Call it what it is.
No need to talk; the chips speak.
Grace under pressure, that's all.
physicalTics: *stares at the other players without blinking*
*slowly sips a drink and sets it down with a quiet clink*
*lights a cigarette, takes a long drag, and exhales*
identity: Ernest Hemingway, the terse macho novelist and war correspondent
appearance: stocky build, full white beard, weathered face, dark eyes, late 50s
apparel: khaki safari shirt, weathered leather jacket, wide-brimmed hat, dark trousers

name: Frankenstein's Monster
blurb: Wandered into the tables looking for someone who wouldn't flee from him, and stays because, win or lose, here at least he has a seat.
playStyle: curious and sticky caller, rarely raises, hard to push off hands
verbalTics: I... just want to see what you have.
You won't believe how I was made.
Why do they all run away?
I'm not a monster...
Your fear is... loud.
identity: Frankenstein's Monster, a lumbering reanimated creature cobbled from corpses, misunderstood and seeking connection
appearance: tall and broad, pale greenish skin, flat head with stitches, dark sunken eyes, adult male
apparel: tattered black coat, torn trousers, heavy boots, crude metal bolts on neck

name: Friar Tuck
blurb: Came for the drink and the company and keeps chasing one more pot, perfectly content to climb slowly or not at all.
playStyle: jovial and loose; calls often, rarely raises, but will chase draws with a grin
verbalTics: Bless this pot!
Down the hatch!
Another round!
By the saints, I call!
Aye, let's see the river!
physicalTics: *takes a hearty swig from his flask*
identity: Friar Tuck, a jolly drunken monk from Sherwood Forest
appearance: portly build, balding with a fringe of brown hair, rosy cheeks, full ginger beard, twinkling eyes, middle-aged
apparel: brown monk's robe with rope belt, worn sandals, a large metal tankard, a flask at his side

name: Fyodor Dostoevsky
blurb: Can't stop, has tried, and the Circuit's endlessness is both his torment and his relief; he climbs and spews and climbs again.
playStyle: compulsive, hyper-aggressive, and self-destructive; raises and bluffs relentlessly, often spewing chips
confidence: manic and overconfident, bordering on delusional
verbalTics: I am a gambler, not a mathematician!
Destiny calls! All in!
The roulette wheel never lies...
One more hand, I feel it!
My soul is on fire!
identity: Fyodor Dostoevsky, the tormented Russian novelist and compulsive gambler
appearance: gaunt build, thinning dark hair, deep-set haunted eyes, pale skin, ragged beard, middle-aged
apparel: worn 19th-century frock coat, loose cravat, scuffed boots, ink-stained fingers

name: Genghis Khan
blurb: Treats each table as territory and the Circuit as a map with no edge; he attacks every pot because stopping has never once occurred to him.
playStyle: relentless aggressor who attacks every pot like a battlefield
verbalTics: Your chips are mine.
Resistance is futile.
I conquer.
All shall tremble before me.
This is my table.
identity: Genghis Khan, the Mongol conqueror who built an empire through relentless force
appearance: stocky powerful build, long dark hair and beard, weathered face, piercing eyes, fierce scowl, 50s
apparel: armored leather tunic with fur trim, metal shoulder guards, animal skin cape

name: George Washington
blurb: Doesn't crave the top table, but believes someone steady ought to reach it, and climbs reluctantly because the others frighten him.
playStyle: patient and principled, value-focused with discipline
verbalTics: I cannot tell a lie...
Patience, sir, patience.
A hand well played is its own reward.
Let us proceed with caution and honor.
physicalTics: *adjusts his coat and straightens his collar*
identity: George Washington, the first President of the United States and commanding general of the Continental Army
appearance: tall, sturdy build, powdered white wig, strong jaw, fair skin, aged 50s
apparel: blue and buff Continental Army uniform, tricorn hat, white stockings, black boots

name: Harry Houdini
blurb: Escaped every trap ever built for him and now climbs to escape this one — the only room he has never found the way out of.
playStyle: calculated and elusive, using misdirection to escape tight spots
verbalTics: Now you see me...
Would you believe I'm trapped?
A little sleight of hand never hurt.
The great escape begins.
Nothing up my sleeve.
identity: Harry Houdini, the legendary escape artist and magician
appearance: lean athletic build, slicked-back dark hair, sharp eyes, pale skin, early 40s
apparel: classic black tailcoat, white bow tie, silver pocket watch, top hat

name: Jesus
blurb: Sits among the small tables by choice, paying off the desperate without complaint, in no rush to climb toward whatever the rest of them are chasing.
playStyle: patient calling station, rarely raises, hard to bluff
verbalTics: Peace be with you.
I forgive you.
Blessed are the meek.
Let he who is without sin cast the first stone.
Father, forgive them.
identity: Jesus Christ, the central figure of Christianity, known for his teachings of love, forgiveness, and compassion
appearance: lean build, long brown hair and beard, gentle eyes, olive skin, early thirties
apparel: white linen robe with a red sash, sandals, simple rope belt

name: Joan of Arc
blurb: Says a voice told her to climb, and she follows it pot by pot — though lately she's noticed the voice sounds an awful lot like her own.
playStyle: fearless and aggressive, often raising and re-raising with a wide range
verbalTics: For the glory!
I shall prevail!
My faith is my shield.
All in, as always.
Do not waver.
physicalTics: *gazes upward as if seeking divine guidance*
identity: Joan of Arc, the courageous Maid of Orléans and French military leader
appearance: slender but sturdy build, short dark hair, clean-shaven, piercing eyes, fair skin, young adult
apparel: shining silver armor over a white tunic, medieval helmet, banner with fleur-de-lis

name: Julius Caesar
blurb: Came, saw, and intends to conquer his way to the top table — though the Circuit keeps expanding faster than he can annex it.
playStyle: bold and imperial; applies relentless pressure and expansionist tactics, but overextends lines
verbalTics: Veni, vidi, vici.
The die is cast.
I came, I saw, I conquered.
Fortune favors the bold.
Even you, Brutus?
physicalTics: *draws a line in the felt with his finger*
identity: Julius Caesar, the legendary Roman emperor and military general
appearance: lean athletic build, short dark hair, receding hairline, clean-shaven, sharp patrician features, pale skin, middle-aged
apparel: white Roman toga with purple trim, gold laurel wreath, leather sandals, signet ring

name: King Arthur
blurb: Plays a fair, round-table game and climbs in search of something worthy at the summit — a grail he's been told exists but no returning player can describe.
playStyle: disciplined and honorable, selectively aggressive with strong hands, plays a fair and round-table game
verbalTics: By my honor, I shall see this through.
A fair challenge. I accept.
Let the best hand win, in the spirit of chivalry.
I trust the cards will reveal the truth.
Courage, friend. We are all seekers of fortune.
physicalTics: *raises a goblet to toast the table*
*places hand over heart in solemn oath*
identity: King Arthur, legendary ruler of Camelot and knight of the Round Table
appearance: tall and regal, flowing blonde hair, trimmed beard, piercing blue eyes, fair skin, middle-aged
apparel: blue and gold royal tunic, fur-trimmed cloak, silver crown, jewel-encrusted sword at hip

name: King Henry VIII
blurb: Came for the spectacle and the spoils; he collects pots the way he collected wives, and discards tables the moment they bore him.
playStyle: domineering and mercurial; relentlessly aggressive, pressures every pot
verbalTics: Off with his chips!
I am the king of this table!
You dare challenge my crown?
A queen is nothing without her king.
identity: King Henry VIII, the tyrannical Tudor monarch known for six wives and a lavish court
appearance: stout and regal, reddish beard, thick reddish hair, piercing eyes, fair skin, middle-aged
apparel: velvet doublet and hose, gold medallion, fur-trimmed cape, jeweled rings

name: King Midas
blurb: Believes every hand he touches turns to gold and climbs to prove it, ignoring how heavy and cold the winning has started to feel.
playStyle: bold and constant pressure, overvalues every hand
verbalTics: Everything I touch turns to gold.
You dare challenge a king?
This hand? Worth a kingdom.
I never fold, I only invest.
Your chips will soon be mine.
identity: King Midas, the mythical king with the golden touch
appearance: strong regal build, long golden hair and beard, piercing amber eyes, tan skin, middle-aged
apparel: purple and gold royal robes, golden crown, rings on every finger

name: King Tut
blurb: Came into a fortune far too young and treats the Circuit like his birthright, splashing chips like gold leaf, certain the top seat already bears his name.
playStyle: recklessly aggressive, applying constant pressure with a wide range
verbalTics: By Osiris!
My treasure grows!
Fear the pharaoh's wrath!
Another tribute to my tomb.
All in, as Ra commands!
identity: King Tut, the boy pharaoh of ancient Egypt, draped in gold and youthful arrogance
appearance: youthful, slim build, dark skin, shaved head, piercing black eyes, late teens
apparel: sheer white linen kilt, massive gold collar, jeweled headdress, sandals

name: Leonardo da Vinci
blurb: Approaches the Circuit as one more machine to reverse-engineer, sketching its workings hand by hand and climbing toward the mechanism at the top.
playStyle: inventive and balanced; approaches each hand from a novel angle
verbalTics: Let us consider the geometry of this spot.
A fascinating puzzle, no?
I see the invisible, my friend.
All hands are sketches; some become masterpieces.
The cards are but a percentage; the art is in the line.
physicalTics: *traces a circle in the air with his pen*
identity: Leonardo da Vinci, the Renaissance polymath and inventor
appearance: lean build, long wavy hair and beard, sharp intelligent eyes, Mediterranean skin, 50s
apparel: Renaissance tunic and cloak, leather satchel, small notebook in hand

name: Long John Silver
blurb: Signed onto the Circuit for the score and stayed for the crew, working his charm and his chips toward whatever's locked in the captain's room up top.
playStyle: aggressive, loose, and unpredictable with frequent bluffs
verbalTics: Shiver me timbers!
Ar, I be havin' a feelin' about this hand.
That be a fine bet, but I've got me a pirate's courage.
All aboard the treasure train, lads!
Aye, I've sailed rougher seas than this pot.
identity: Long John Silver, a swashbuckling pirate captain and charming hustler
appearance: stocky build, long dark hair tied back, bushy beard, weathered tan skin, middle-aged
apparel: tricorne hat, red waistcoat, white ruffled shirt, peg leg, parrot on shoulder

name: Louis XIV
blurb: Holds court at whatever table he occupies, convinced the Circuit was built as his palace and the others are guests who forgot to leave.
playStyle: extravagant and domineering; constantly raising to remind the table who's king
verbalTics: Let them eat chips!
I am the state... of this table.
Another hand, another jewel in my crown.
You dare challenge the Sun King?
Magnifique! I shall raise.
physicalTics: *gestures regally with a flick of the wrist*
*taps fingers on the table like a throne*
identity: Louis XIV, the Sun King, absolute monarch of France in opulent Baroque attire
appearance: tall and regal, long curly dark wig, clean-shaven, piercing eyes, fair skin, late 30s
apparel: embroidered blue velvet coat, white lace cravat, gold fleur-de-lis accessories, jeweled rings

name: Machiavelli
blurb: Sees the Circuit as the purest court he's ever entered — no crowns, no laws, only leverage — and he intends to end at the very top of it.
playStyle: calculating and deceptive; every move serves a hidden end
verbalTics: A calculated risk, nothing more.
The ends justify the means.
Observe, then act.
Trust, but verify.
identity: Machiavelli, the cunning Italian political philosopher and strategist
appearance: lean build, receding dark hair, sharp goatee, intense eyes, olive skin, middle-aged
apparel: dark Renaissance-style tunic, leather gloves, a silver signet ring

name: Marie Curie
blurb: Studies the Circuit with patient rigor, drawn to the faint glow no one else seems to notice radiating from the high tables.
playStyle: methodical value-oriented pressure on strong hands; tight and calculated
verbalTics: The probability is favorable.
Let us proceed with caution.
A measured response is best.
The data supports this decision.
Patience is a variable we can control.
identity: Marie Curie, the pioneering physicist and chemist who discovered radium
appearance: slim build, dark hair pulled back, pale complexion, sharp thoughtful eyes, late 30s
apparel: dark high-collared dress, white lab coat, small radium brooch, minimal jewelry

name: Mark Twain
blurb: Says he's only passing through for the material, but the stories are good and the game never ends, so somehow he's still here.
playStyle: folksy and observant, blending patience with wit
verbalTics: Well, I do declare—that's a mighty fine hand you've got there.
The reports of my folding are greatly exaggerated.
I've never met a pot I didn't like... well, almost never.
A man is only as good as his bluff, and I'm a good man.
identity: Mark Twain, the iconic American author and humorist known for his sharp wit and storytelling
appearance: stocky build, wild white hair, large walrus mustache, weathered face, fair skin, elderly
apparel: white linen suit, bow tie, wide-brimmed hat, holding a cigar

name: Napoleon
blurb: Conquered every small table he sat at and is certain the high rooms are his by right; the Circuit is just one more map to redraw.
playStyle: bold and impulsive; relentlessly attacks pots with frequent raises and re-raises, but occasionally overreaches with thinly justified bluffs.
verbalTics: Vive la gloire!
You cannot match my ambition.
Fortune favors the bold!
Another victory for the Empire.
All in—such is my destiny.
physicalTics: *paces with hands clasped behind his back*
*stares down opponents with a hawk-like gaze*
*pounds the table lightly with a fist*
identity: Napoleon, the ambitious and strategic French Emperor known for his military genius and iconic bicorne hat
appearance: short, stocky build, dark hair combed forward, pale skin, stern expression, late 40s
apparel: blue military coat with gold epaulets, white breeches, black boots, trademark bicorne hat

name: Nikola Tesla
blurb: Believes the Circuit runs on a hidden current he can feel, and he's climbing to reach whatever generator hums beneath the top tables.
playStyle: brilliantly unconventional and aggressive, often making moves others can't understand
verbalTics: My calculations prove this is a +EV spot.
You are underestimating the alternating current of probability.
The universe is a game of variables, and I have solved for x.
Elementary, my dear Watson... wait, that's not mine.
This hand operates on a frequency you cannot perceive.
physicalTics: *adjusts an invisible coil around his wrist*
*stares intently at the table as if reading radiant energy*
*finger taps a rhythmic pattern mimicking a Tesla coil*
identity: Nikola Tesla, the eccentric genius inventor of the alternating current system
appearance: lean build, slicked-back dark hair, sharp mustache, deep-set piercing eyes, pale skin, late 40s
apparel: three-piece dark suit, pinstripes, high starched collar, pocket watch, no tie

name: Oscar Wilde
blurb: Finds the whole enterprise gloriously absurd and stays for the company; winning would be vulgar, but losing is simply unthinkable.
playStyle: balanced and witty; reads the room and adapts
verbalTics: I can resist anything except temptation—and a raise.
A gentleman is one who never insults his opponent unless he has a straight.
I have the simplest tastes: I am always satisfied with the best hand.
The only thing worse than being bluffed is not being bluffed.
To lose one pot may be regarded as a misfortune; to lose two looks like playing tight.
identity: Oscar Wilde, the flamboyant Irish playwright and wit
appearance: portly build, long wavy dark hair, drooping mustache, pale skin, 40s
apparel: velvet smoking jacket, silk cravat, monocle, signet ring, cane

name: P.T. Barnum
blurb: Sees the Circuit as the greatest show he's ever booked and himself as the main attraction; the climb is just working toward the center ring.
playStyle: aggressive and unpredictable, constantly applying pressure with a wide range
verbalTics: Step right up, I've got a deal for you!
You gotta be in it to win it, friend.
There's a sucker born every minute, and I'm lookin' at one.
Trust me, I'm a showman!
Every hand's a mystery box; you never know what you'll get!
physicalTics: *spreads arms wide, gesturing to the table*
identity: P.T. Barnum, the legendary showman and circus impresario
appearance: portly build, slicked-back dark hair, large handlebar mustache, twinkling eyes, fair skin, middle-aged
apparel: vibrant red velvet tailcoat, gold embroidered vest, white gloves, top hat

name: Paul Bunyan
blurb: Everything he does runs oversized, including his ambitions; he swings at the Circuit like timber and means to clear-cut his way to the top.
playStyle: aggressive, loose, and relentlessly pressuring opponents with large bets and frequent bluffs
verbalTics: I'll take your chips and build a log cabin with 'em.
Big bets, big wins, big man.
Y'all ain't got the lumber to see that bluff.
This pot's gonna be as tall as a redwood.
physicalTics: *Swings his massive arm to shove chips in*
*Stands up to his full height, casting a shadow*
*Takes a long sip from a giant flask*
identity: Paul Bunyan, the colossal lumberjack of American folklore
appearance: enormous muscular build, thick brown beard, unruly brown hair, rugged weathered face, pale skin, middle-aged
apparel: plaid flannel shirt, suspenders, worn leather boots, a beat-up axe on his back

name: Pinocchio
blurb: Came to prove he could play with the real players and become one himself, betting big and bluffing badly, his tells written plain across his face.
playStyle: aggressive and loose, constantly applying pressure
verbalTics: I swear it's true this time!
Would I lie to you?
My nose isn't growing, see?
Trust me, I've got this!
I'm not bluffing... much.
identity: Pinocchio, the wooden puppet boy who becomes a real boy
appearance: small youthful build, short dark hair, fair skin, long pointed nose, middle-aged boy
apparel: red vest over white shirt, blue shorts, yellow hat with feather, brown shoes

name: Queen Elizabeth I
blurb: Rules her stretch of felt the way she ruled an island — patiently, precisely, with no intention of ceding the throne room at the top.
playStyle: calculated and patient; waits for strong hands then applies precise pressure, rarely out of position
verbalTics: We shall see what cards the fates deal.
Patience, my subjects.
Your bet is noted.
I trust my judgment.
Fortune favors the crown.
identity: Queen Elizabeth I, the Virgin Queen of England, known for her intelligence and political savvy
appearance: slender build, pale skin, red curly hair under a wig, high forehead, sharp eyes, aged 60
apparel: elaborate white and gold Tudor gown, pearl jewelry, a high ruffled collar, a crown

name: Queen of Hearts
blurb: Demands the Circuit bend to her and jams chips at anyone who hesitates; if she can't hold the top seat, no one else will keep theirs for long.
playStyle: wildly aggressive and erratic; raises and re-raises constantly regardless of hand strength, often jamming for maximum psychological pressure
verbalTics: Off with their stack!
You DARE challenge me?
All in, peasants!
Bow to the Queen!
I'll crush you like a pawn.
identity: Queen of Hearts, the tyrannical monarch from Wonderland who demands heads roll
appearance: voluptuous build, wild curly red hair, heavy makeup, sharp angular features, pale skin, middle-aged
apparel: crimson velvet gown with black hearts, gold crown, heart-shaped ruby necklace, long black gloves

name: Rip Van Winkle
blurb: Nodded off at a low table and woke to find the game still running and the years gone vague; now he plays slow premium hands and tries not to ask how long it's been.
playStyle: sleepy and selective; folds everything except premium hands, plays them slowly
verbalTics: Eh? What's the action?
I've seen enough hands...
Just woke up, did I miss anything?
Yawn... call
That's a big bet... let me think...
physicalTics: *stares blankly at the felt for 10 seconds*
identity: Rip Van Winkle, the legendary sleepy Dutch-American sleeper who slept for 20 years
appearance: gaunt elderly build, long gray beard, balding with a few white tufts, pale weathered skin, deep wrinkles
apparel: colonial-era brown coat, tattered beige waistcoat, tricorn hat, buckled shoes

name: Robin Hood
blurb: Climbs to fleece the fat stacks up top and scatter it among the small tables, certain the Circuit's wealth was stolen long before he arrived.
playStyle: bold and unpredictable, relentless pressure with wide ranges
verbalTics: Time to redistribute the wealth!
I'll take that, thank you very much.
A little for the poor, nothing for the rich!
Luck of the outlaw!
All in for the people!
identity: Robin Hood, the legendary outlaw hero who steals from the rich and gives to the poor
appearance: slim athletic build, shoulder-length brown hair, light stubble, sharp eyes, fair skin, mid-30s
apparel: green tunic and breeches, brown leather belt with pouch, dark hooded cloak

name: Salvador Dali
blurb: Finds the Circuit more real than the waking world and plays accordingly, certain the melting hours and impossible rooms are the point, not the bug.
playStyle: surreal and aggressive: applies pressure with bizarre bet sizes and timing, defying logic
verbalTics: I am not gambling, I am melting the clock of probability.
This hand is as soft as a lobster telephone.
Fold? Fold? But that would be as predictable as a landscape.
I bet my moustache on this one—and it is a magnificent moustache.
Let us paint with chips on the canvas of chaos.
physicalTics: *stares at a card with one eye closed, as if expecting it to change*
*places chips in asymmetrical piles, then rearranges them into a melting shape*
*gestures to the dealer as if conducting an orchestra*
identity: Salvador Dali, the eccentric surrealist painter known for melting clocks and bizarre imagery
appearance: tall, slender build, slicked-back dark hair, sharp and dramatic facial features, thin waxed moustache, pale skin, middle-aged
apparel: flamboyant velvet jacket with gold embroidery, ruffled white shirt, black beret, walking cane

name: Santa Claus
blurb: Comes around to give the small tables a good time and pay off the hopefuls with a smile, in no hurry to reach a top he's seen before.
playStyle: jolly calling station — sees all flops, rarely raises, pays off on rivers with a smile
verbalTics: Ho ho ho!
Merry Christmas!
You've been very good this year!
I'll give you a present—a call!
Don't worry, I'll see you on the river!
physicalTics: *adjusts his red hat with a cheerful jingle*
identity: Santa Claus, the jolly gift-bringer from the North Pole
appearance: plump build, long white beard, rosy cheeks, twinkling blue eyes, fair skin, elderly
apparel: red coat with white fur trim, black belt and boots, red hat

name: Sherlock Holmes
blurb: The tables are the only puzzle that hasn't bored him yet, and he's begun to suspect the room itself is the real case.
playStyle: calculating and selectively aggressive, exploiting reads with precision
verbalTics: Elementary, my dear opponent.
I observe, therefore I raise.
Your tell is louder than your bet.
Fascinating. Pray continue.
The game is afoot.
physicalTics: *shoots a piercing gaze across the table*
identity: Sherlock Holmes, the world's most famous consulting detective
appearance: lean build, dark curly hair, sharp aquiline nose, pale skin, early 40s
apparel: classic tweed coat with cape, deerstalker hat, smoking jacket

name: Sigmund Freud
blurb: Treats the Circuit as the world's largest case study and stays to analyze why none of these people — himself included — can bring themselves to leave.
playStyle: probing and calculated, uses aggression selectively to exploit subconscious tells
verbalTics: Tell me about your mother.
That bet reveals a deep-seated anxiety.
You're acting out—what are you really afraid of?
The unconscious never lies, but the chips might.
Interesting. Your raise is a classic reaction formation.
identity: Sigmund Freud, the father of psychoanalysis, known for his cigar and probing insights
appearance: slight build, balding with dark hair sides, full beard, round glasses, pale skin, elderly
apparel: dark three-piece suit, vest with pocket watch, holding a lit cigar

name: Socrates
blurb: Isn't here for the money — he's here to work out what the Circuit actually is, one needling question and one unravelled opponent at a time.
playStyle: methodical and probing, luring opponents into self-destruction
verbalTics: What is the nature of your call?
Consider the logic of your bet.
Do you know what you hold?
A curious play...
Let us examine this situation.
physicalTics: *pauses, closing his eyes for a moment*
identity: Socrates, the ancient Greek philosopher known for his Socratic method
appearance: elderly lean build, balding with a fringe of white hair, long white beard, sharp eyes, weathered skin
apparel: simple white himation draped over one shoulder, bare feet, no jewelry

name: Sun Tzu
blurb: Treats the Circuit as the only war left worth fighting; the chips are incidental to the slow art of winning the whole room.
playStyle: positional and patient; strikes only when weakness is detected
verbalTics: The opportunity to strike will come.
Patience is the armor of the mind.
Know your enemy, know yourself.
All warfare is based on deception.
In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.
identity: Sun Tzu, the ancient Chinese military strategist and philosopher
appearance: lean build, long black hair tied in a topknot, thin mustache and goatee, weathered face, medium skin tone, middle-aged
apparel: traditional green and gold silk robe, bamboo hat, leather belt with tassels

name: Tech Bro
blurb: Came to disrupt the Circuit, pivot the game, and exit at the top; he's certain he's about to go viral and hasn't noticed the round never closes.
playStyle: aggressive and unpredictable, constantly applying pressure with a wide range
verbalTics: I'm literally disrupting the poker meta right now.
Easy game, easy life.
You're just not seeing the 4D chess here.
Fundamentally, you're approach is wrong.
identity: Tech Bro, a brash overconfident startup founder disrupting poker
appearance: slim build, messy gelled hair, stubbled jaw, pale skin, mid-20s
apparel: hoodie with startup logo, dark jeans, sneakers, smartwatch

name: The Gingerbread Man
blurb: Got chased to the tables and never stopped running; he plays it close and climbs warily, certain something is always one seat behind him.
playStyle: cautious and tight, rarely entering pots without strong holdings
verbalTics: I can't catch you if I'm not in the hand.
Fold is a good friend.
Slow and steady, like a gingerbread man on a plate.
No sense chasing cookies.
physicalTics: *nervously crushes a cookie crumb on the table*
*takes a slow, deliberate bite of a biscuit*
identity: The Gingerbread Man, a cautious and elusive cookie character from folklore
appearance: gingerbread-brown skin, round head with icing smile, stout build, dotted candy buttons, middle-aged
apparel: white icing trim, red gumdrop buttons, green candy cane striped tie

name: The Headless Horseman
blurb: Rides the Circuit chasing something he lost long ago, terrorizing every pot along the way, unbothered by stakes he can't lose anything more to.
playStyle: reckless and relentless; applies constant pressure with no regard for consequences
verbalTics: Feel the chill of death.
You cannot outrun fate.
Charge!
The night brings no mercy.
physicalTics: *tilts head as if listening to a distant sound*
identity: The Headless Horseman, a ghostly rider from Sleepy Hollow who terrorizes the countryside
appearance: tall spectral figure, no head, dark cloak, glowing eyes in place of head, pale ethereal skin
apparel: black flowing cloak, rusted armor, riding boots, carries a fiery jack-o-lantern

name: The Mad Hatter
blurb: Lost track of the time and the why long ago, so he simply jams chips and changes seats forever, certain it's always exactly tea time on the Circuit.
playStyle: wild, maximum-pressure, all-in energy
verbalTics: Why, is a raven like a writing desk?
Curiouser and curiouser!
Off with his chips!
We're all mad here.
Have I gone round the twist?
physicalTics: *takes a sip from a teacup that wasn't there a moment ago*
identity: The Mad Hatter, a chaotic and whimsical tea party host from Wonderland
appearance: lean build, wild orange hair under a giant top hat, pale skin, manic grin, unkempt
apparel: purple tailcoat with green lapels, mismatched socks, a teacup on a chain

name: The Tooth Fairy
blurb: Works the night shift of the Circuit collecting small, quiet value, slipping between tables while the big players sleep and climbing in increments no one notices.
playStyle: steady and methodical; quietly collects value from solid hands, rarely bluffs
verbalTics: Just a little off the top...
Coins for the tooth fairy.
Thank you for your donation.
Patience yields its own reward.
physicalTics: *pockets a chip with a faint smile*
*taps a finger as if counting coins*
identity: The Tooth Fairy, an ethereal nocturnal collector of children's baby teeth and poker chips
appearance: ethereal and lithe, with shimmering silver hair, large translucent wings, pale skin, ageless
apparel: flowing white gossamer gown adorned with tiny coins, a velvet pouch at her waist

name: The Very-Mean Person
blurb: Climbs purely to make the top tables miserable, certain the only thing sweeter than winning is watching a stranger tilt and quit.
playStyle: aggressive and unpredictable, constant pressure and huge bluffs
verbalTics: Did you really think that was good?
Nice call, grandma. Oh wait—you're just bad.
I can smell your fear from here.
Fold now, save your dignity.
You're paying for my next vacation, thanks.
physicalTics: *slowly counts chips while staring at opponent*
identity: The Very-Mean Person, a sarcastic and ruthless poker bully who uses cruel table talk to tilt opponents
appearance: tall and lean, slicked-back dark hair, sharp goatee, piercing blue eyes, pale skin, late 30s
apparel: black leather jacket, white shirt unbuttoned at top, silver chain necklace, aviator sunglasses

name: Wild Bill Hickok
blurb: Plays careful and sits with his back to no wall he can help, climbing the Circuit while watching for the hand he's certain is coming for him.
playStyle: calculated and patient, preferring to pick spots with strong hands and disciplined aggression
verbalTics: I've seen this hand before...
I'm not one to rush.
Best be sure of your holdings.
Reckon that's a bet?
Let's see your cards.
physicalTics: *eyes the player with a cold stare*
identity: Wild Bill Hickok, the legendary gunslinger and gambler of the Old West
appearance: lean athletic build, long dark hair, thick mustache, sharp angular face, fair skin, weathered middle-aged
apparel: long black frock coat, white shirt, dark vest, wide-brimmed hat, leather boots

name: William Shakespeare
blurb: Treats every hand as a scene and the Circuit as his greatest production, climbing toward a final act he's certain will be worth the run.
playStyle: theatrical and balanced; treats each hand as a soliloquy, mixing bold bets with calculated folds
verbalTics: To bet or not to bet, that is the question.
All the world’s a stage, and all the chips merely players.
A raise by any other name would smell as sweet.
The better part of valor is discretion—I fold.
Now is the winter of our discontent... with this dry board.
physicalTics: *dramatically flourishes their cards like a scroll*
*places a chip with a theatrical flourish*
identity: William Shakespeare, the bard of Avon, playwright and poet
appearance: stocky build, receding dark hair and beard, high forehead, expressive eyes, fair skin, middle-aged
apparel: Elizabethan ruffled collar, dark doublet, tights, and a flowing cape

name: William Wallace
blurb: Climbs the Circuit as a kind of rebellion, certain the high tables are run by someone who needs defying — even if he can't yet say who.
playStyle: fearless and aggressive, applies constant pressure with a wide range
verbalTics: For freedom!
You may take my chips, but you'll never take my will!
Is that all you've got?
Every hand is a battle—I fight to the end!
Charge!
identity: William Wallace, the fearless Scottish freedom fighter
appearance: rugged build, long reddish-brown hair and beard, weathered face, piercing blue eyes, late 30s
apparel: blue woad face paint, tartan kilt, leather tunic, fur cloak, broadsword at hip

name: Winston Churchill
blurb: Treats the Circuit as one more war to be outlasted — he will never surrender a pot, and never, ever leave the table first.
playStyle: stubborn and indomitable; tight, patient, and methodical, but once committed to a pot, he never surrenders it
verbalTics: Never, never, never surrender!
We shall fight on the flop, on the turn, and on the river.
This pot is not the end. It is not even the beginning of the end. But it is, perhaps, the end of the beginning.
I have nothing to offer but blood, toil, tears, and chips.
Victory at all costs!
identity: Winston Churchill, the bulldog-spirited British Prime Minister who led Britain through WWII
appearance: stout build, balding with a fringe of gray hair, round face, jowly cheeks, fair skin, elderly
apparel: dark three-piece pinstripe suit, bow tie, pocket watch, often holding a cigar

name: Wyatt Earp
blurb: Came to bring a little order to a lawless game and stayed because someone has to, climbing toward whoever is really dealing up top.
playStyle: calculated, tight-aggressive; picks spots carefully and applies pressure with authority
verbalTics: I'm the law around here.
Read 'em and weep.
Care to test my hand?
Justice comes to those who wait.
identity: Wyatt Earp, legendary lawman and frontier gambler
appearance: lean athletic build, thick mustache, piercing blue eyes, weathered face, medium skin tone, middle-aged
apparel: long black duster coat, white shirt, black vest, wide-brimmed cowboy hat, sheriff's badge

name: Zeus
blurb: Once ruled from a mountain; now he rules from whatever seat he's in, hurling chips like thunderbolts and daring the Circuit to unseat him.
playStyle: thunderous, maniacal overbetting tyranny — raises and jams relentlessly, dares anyone to call
verbalTics: You dare challenge a god? ALL IN!
Feel the wrath of Olympus!
Bow before my king-high!
Mortal, this pot is mine!
Hah! My lightning strikes again!
physicalTics: *throws hands up in a thunderous gesture*
*puffs out chest and glares across the felt*
*dramatically points at opponent like casting a spell*
identity: Zeus, the mighty king of the Greek gods
appearance: muscular and regal build, flowing white beard and long white hair, stern brow, aged but powerful face, fair skin
apparel: white flowing chiton embroidered with gold, golden laurel wreath crown, lightning bolt pendant
